steps.title: Use difference of squares
steps.content: Now factor the bracket: $$x^2-(x+12)^2=(x-(x+12))(x+(x+12))$$ $$=(-12)(2x+12)$$ So $2x+12$ is a factor.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The expression with root $-6$ is $2x+12$, because $2(-6)+12=0$. The other factor from the difference of squares is the constant $-12$, so $2x+12$ is the useful linear factor. Therefore, B is correct.
